(#612) (0681a72)\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eBuild Plugins: End of support for Node.js 18 + Node.js 20 - Updates - Netlify Support Forums\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://answers.netlify.com/t/build-plugins-end-of-support-for-node-js-18-node-js-20/162662\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eThe following versions of Node.js have reached their official end of life:\n...\n- Node.js v18 on April 30, 2025\n- Node.js v20 on April 30, 2026\n...\nNow it’s time to say goodbye to Node.js versions 18 and 20 in our build plugins. This change will allow us to use newer Node.js features inside our codebase and apply important security fixes.\n...\nWe will no longer support Node.js v18 or v20 for Build Plugins starting on June 16, 2026.\n...\n- Running a build with Node.js version lower than 22.12.x is still supported, but upgrading to a more recent and supported version of Node.js is strongly recommended.\n- If you run your build with Node.js version lower than 22.12.x, local plugins and plugins installed in a site’s package.json will automatically start using our system default Node.js version instead (v22 as of February 24th 2025).\n- If you run your build with Node.js version 22.12.x or above nothing will change - your plugins will continue to be executed with the version they use now.\n...\nAlmost all plugins are already compatible with Node.js v22 but you may want to confirm this for your plugin. If your plugin is not compatible, you will need to migrate your plugin to support Node.js v22 before June 16, 2026.\n...\nReview and upgrade your plugins to ensure they are compatible with Node.js v22 or file an issue on the plugin’s repository so that the plugin author can make the necessary changes.\n...\n- Setting the Node version for site builds\n- Manage plugin versions\n- Builds + Functions + Plugins: Default Node.js version upgrade to 22\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eBuilds + Functions + Plugins: Default Node.js version upgrade to 22 - Updates - Netlify Support Forums\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://answers.netlify.com/t/builds-functions-plugins-default-node-js-version-upgrade-to-22/135981\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Builds + Functions + Plugins: Default Node.js version\n...\nto 22\n...\nThe default system Node.js version will be updated from version v18 → v22 on January 27, 2025.\n...\nIMPORTANT UPDATE - This release been delayed to February 24, 2025\n...\nNode.js v22 is the current LTS (Long Term Supported) version, and updating to use Node.js v22 by default allows customers to take advantage of the latest features as well as benefit from the performance and security improvements that the newer version delivers.\n...\n- The default Node.js version available on the build image will be v22\n- The default runtime for Node.js functions deployed after this change will be v22\n- Plugins installed through the Integrations tab will begin using v22.\n- Local plugins and plugins installed via a site’s&#96;package.json&#96; that use a Node.js version lower than v18 will default to using v22.\n...\nNote - Running the build command itself is unaffected by this change, and running it with Node.js versions below v22 continues to be supported.\n...\nPlugin authors: Almost all plugins are already compatible with Node.js v22 but you may want to confirm this for your plugin. If your plugin is not compatible, you will need to migrate your plugin to support Node.js v22 before February 24, 2025.\n...\nPlugin users: If you are using a plugin that no longer works after the upgrade to Node.js v22, please file an issue on the plugin’s repository so that the plugin author can make the necessary changes.\n...\nIMPORTANT UPDATE The date of this release has been delayed from January 27, 2025 to February 24, 2025 in order to give everyone a little more time to update their sites and to prepare for this change.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eThe Node.js version is 22.17.1 but the plugin \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\" requires \u003e=14.15 \u003c20 - Support - Netlify Support Forums\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://answers.netlify.com/t/the-node-js-version-is-22-17-1-but-the-plugin-netlify-plugin-lighthouse-requires-14-15-20/154962\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eThe Node.js version is 22.17.1 but the plugin \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\" requires \u003e=14.15 \u003c20 - Support - Netlify Support Forums\n...\n# The Node.js version is 22.17.1 but the plugin \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\" requires \u003e=14.15 \u003c20\n...\nI get the error message on building the site. The Node.js version is 22.17.1 but the plugin “@netlify/plugin-lighthouse” requires \u003e=14.15 \u003c20\n...\nI have updated node version in dependency managment under continuous deployment to \u003c20 but see an error Node.js version 18.x has reached end-of-life. We recommend using Node.js 22.x, the current LTS version, instead.\n...\n10\n...\n10:\n...\nui 10:30:04 AM: package: “@netlify/plugin-lighthouse” 10:30:04 AM: Build failed due to a user error: Build script returned non-zero exit code:\n...\n2 10:30:04 AM: Failing build: Failed to build site 10:30:04 AM: Finished processing build request in 5m26.0\n...\n@mjneary10 Update the version of&#96;@netlify/plugin-lighthouse&#96; being used.\n...\nIf it’s saying&#96;\u003e=14.15 \u003c20&#96; then it must be a version before these Jan 2024 changes:\n...\n#### feat!: mark compatible with Node v20 and up (#612)\n...\n* feat!: mark compatible with Node v20 and up * update node version for build…* update ci * update nvmrc\n...\nThe latest version is&#96;6.0.1&#96;:\n...\n### @netlify/plugin-lighthouse\n...\nNetlify Plugin to run Lighthouse on each build. Latest version: 6.0.1, last published: 9 months ago. Start using @netlify/plugin-lighthouse in your project by running &#96;npm i @netlify/plugin-lighthouse&#96;. There are 0 other projects in the npm registry...\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n9:45:53 AM:   The Node.js version is 22.17.1 but the plugin \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\" requires \u003e=14.15 \u003c20\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n...\nI’d interpreted the main issue as that plugin being unable to run with Node.js 20+, which was addressed some time ago.\n...\nexpect it to respect the Node.js version\n...\nhrishikesh August 18, 2025, 1:07pm 5\n\nPlugins don’t use user-defined Node.js versions. They use the system Node.js version which currently is Node 22.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The build failure is caused by `@netlify/plugin-lighthouse@4`, installed through the Netlify UI Integrations tab.

Version 4 requires Node < 20, but the build environment uses Node 22. Updating the plugin to version 6.x resolves this, since it supports Node 20+. The plan is to pin the plugin version in `netlify.toml`.

The exact `netlify.toml` entry is `package = "@netlify/plugin-lighthouse@6"`. This overrides the UI-installed version 4.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

[netlify/netlify-plugin-lighthouse](https://github.com/netlify/netlify-plugin-lighthouse)\n3. [](https://docs.netlify.com/extend/install-and-use/build-plugins/)\n4. [Enable Lighthouse Reports with web vitals for each deploy | Netlify Blog](https://www.netlify.com/blog/lighthouse-plugin-full-reports-for-each-deploy/)\n5. [Now available: manage Build Plugin versions - Updates - Netlify Support Forums](https://answers.netlify.com/t/now-available-manage-build-plugin-versions/42293)\n\nSynthesis:\nTo use @netlify/plugin-lighthouse version 6 in your project, first install it as a development dependency by running npm install -D @netlify/plugin-lighthouse in your terminal [1][2]. Then, add the plugin to your netlify.toml file [1][3]:\n\n[[plugins]]\n package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"\n\n [plugins.inputs.thresholds]\n performance = 0.9\n\nThis configuration allows you to set performance thresholds and customize audit behavior [1][4]. Updates to the plugin are managed via your package.json devDependencies [3][5].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e@netlify/plugin-lighthouse v6.0.4\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.npmjs.com/package/@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# @netlify/plugin-lighthouse v6.0.4\n...\n- Version: 6.0.4\n- Homepage: https://github.com/netlify/netlify-plugin-lighthouse#readme\n- Repository: git+https://github.com/netlify/netlify-plugin-lighthouse.git\n- Author: netlify-labs\n- Bugs: https://github.com/netlify/netlify-plugin-lighthouse/issues\n- Keywords: netlify, netlify-plugin, lighthouse\n- License: MIT\n- Maintainers:\n...\n\u003csean.roberts90@gmail.com\u003e, biilmann \u003cinfo@mathias-biilmann.net\u003e, eduardoboucas \u003cmail@eduardoboucas.com\u003e, netlify-bot \u003cbot@netlify.com\u003e, sarahetter \u003csarah.etter@netlify.com\u003e, mikewen \u003cmike.wen@netlify\n...\ncom\u003e, kathmbeck \u003ckatherine.beck@netlify.com\u003e, hrishikeshk \u003chrishi5200@gmail.com\u003e, vitaliyr \u003cvit@ribachenko.com\u003e, berdav \u003cdavid@stackbit\n...\ncom\u003e, youvalv \u003cyouval.vaknin@gmail\n...\ncom\u003e, serhalp-\n...\n\u003cphilippe.serhal@\n...\ndomitriusaclark@gmail.com\u003e, akardet \u003csam@netlify.com\u003e, tbarn \u003ctaylorbarnett42@gmail.com\u003e, seancdavis \u003cscdavis41@\n...\nYou can install the plugin for your site using your &#96;netlify.toml&#96; file or the Netlify UI.\n...\nFor the most customization options, we recommend installing the Lighthouse plugin with a &#96;netlify.toml&#96; file.\n...\n### Install plugin with a &#96;netlify.toml&#96; file\n...\nThen add the plugin to your &#96;netlify.toml&#96; configuration file:\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;toml\n[[plugins]]\n  package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"\n\n  # optional, deploy the lighthouse report to a path under your site\n  [plugins.inputs.audits]\n    output_path = \"reports/lighthouse.html\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n...\npackage = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\n...\nnetlify.toml\n...\n\"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\n...\n| Versions | Published | Releases | Deps |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| 6.0.0 - 6.0.4 | Jan 2024 - Nov 2025 | 5 | 8 |\n| 5.0.0 | Jul 13, 2023 | 1 | 8 |\n| 4.1.0 - 4.1.1 | Apr 2023 | 2 | 8 |\n| 4.0.0 - 4.0.7 | Oct 2022 - Jan 2023 | 8 | 8 |\n| 3.7.0 - 3.7.1 | Oct 2022 | 2 | 8 |\n| 3.6.0 | Sep 30, 2022 | 1 | 8 |\n| 3.5.0 | Sep 29, 2022 | 1 | 8 |\n| 3.4.0 - 3.4.1 | Sep 2022 | 2 | 8 |\n| 3.3.0 | Sep 9, 2022 | 1 | 8 |\n| 3.2.0 - 3.2.1 | Aug 2022 | 2 | 8 |\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003enetlify/netlify-plugin-lighthouse\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/netlify/netlify-plugin-lighthouse\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eYou can install the plugin for your site using your &#96;netlify.toml&#96; file or the Netlify UI.\n...\nFor the most customization options, we recommend installing the Lighthouse plugin with a &#96;netlify.toml&#96; file.\n...\nInstall plugin with a &#96;netlify.toml&#96; file\n...\nFrom your project's base directory, use npm, yarn, or any other Node.js package manager to add the plugin to &#96;devDependencies&#96; in &#96;package.json&#96;.\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpm install -D @netlify/plugin-lighthouse\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n...\nThen add the plugin to your &#96;netlify.toml&#96; configuration file:\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;toml\n[[plugins]]\n  package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"\n\n  # optional, deploy the lighthouse report to a path under your site\n  [plugins.inputs.audits]\n    output_path = \"reports/lighthouse.html\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;toml\n[[plugins]]\n  package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"\n\n  # Set minimum thresholds for each report area\n  [plugins.inputs.thresholds]\n    performance = 0.9\n\n  # to audit a path other than /\n  # route1 audit will use the top level thresholds\n  [[plugins.inputs.audits]]\n    path = \"route1\"\n\n    # you can optionally specify an output_path per audit, relative to the path, where HTML report output will be saved\n    output_path = \"reports/route1.html\"\n\n  # to audit a specific absolute url\n  [[plugins.inputs.audits]]\n    url = \"https://www.example.com\"\n\n    # you can specify thresholds per audit\n    [plugins.inputs.audits.thresholds]\n      performance = 0.8\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;toml\n[[plugins]]\n  package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"\n\n  #\n...\nto run prior to deploy, failing the build if minimum thresholds aren't set\n...\nfail_deploy_\n...\n_score_thresholds\n...\naudits for the\n...\nrun Lighthouse audits for\n...\n&#96;preset = \"\n...\nyour &#96;netlify.toml&#96; file:\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;toml\n[[plugins]]\n  package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"\n\n  [plugins.inputs.settings]\n    preset = \"desktop\" # Optionally run Lighthouse using a desktop configuration\n...\nto &#96;netlify.toml&#96;\n...\ntake effect for new builds\n...\n= \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://docs.netlify.com/extend/install-and-use/build-plugins/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eIn both cases, you [configure settings](#configure-settings) in &#96;netlify.toml&#96;. For a plugin published to npm, you also [add it as a dependency](#add-dependency). Then you can [test or run](#run-and-test) the plugin as part of a build.\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;toml\n# Configuration for a plugin published to npm\n[[plugins]]\npackage = \"netlify-plugin-lighthouse\"\n\n  [plugins.inputs]\n  output_path = \"reports/lighthouse.html\"\n...\nEach &#96;[[plugins]]&#96; entry accepts two keys:\n...\n- **&#96;package&#96;** (required)**:** \n - for a plugin installed from npm, the npm package name of the plugin. \n - for a [local plugin](/extend/develop-and-share/develop-build-plugins#local-plugins), the absolute path to a directory containing the plugin's &#96;index.js&#96; and &#96;manifest.yml&#96; files. The &#96;package&#96; value for a local plugin must start with &#96;.&#96; or &#96;/&#96;.\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;toml\n...\n.\n[[\n...\n]]\npackage = \"@netlify/\n...\nNetlify encourages plugin authors to regularly update functionality and release new versions using [semantic versioning](https://docs.npmjs.com/about-semantic-versioning). Minor plugin version updates introduce only backward compatible new features, while major plugin version updates can introduce breaking changes. Refer to the plugin's changelog, linked from the **Options** menu for the plugin listing in the Netlify UI, for version details.\n...\nFor plugins [installed in the UI](#ui-installation) or [installed automatically](#automatic-installation), Netlify updates your site for minor plugin version releases automatically. To manage major plugin updates for a site, take the following steps:\n...\nSubsequent builds\n...\nuse the plugin version that you've chosen and confirmed.\n...\nFor plugins [installed through file-based installation](#file-based-installation), you can [manage versions](https://docs.npmjs.com/about-semantic-versioning#using-semantic-versioning-to-specify-update-types-your-package-can-accept) in your site's &#96;package.json&#96; file under &#96;devDependencies&#96;.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEnable Lighthouse Reports with web vitals for each deploy | Netlify Blog\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.netlify.com/blog/lighthouse-plugin-full-reports-for-each-deploy/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eThe performance of your site or application may be different based on a user’s device type or location. By default, without any configurations to the .toml file, Netlify will run the Lighthouse test from a mobile device type with the locale “en”.\n...\nNow you have the option to configure different configurations for your Lighthouse test results within the Netlify .toml file.\n...\nFor example, if you’d like to change the default device type from “mobile” to “desktop” you can write a line in the .toml file that looks like this:\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[[plugins]]  package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"  [plugins.inputs.settings]    preset = \"desktop\" # Optionally run Lighthouse using a desktop configuration\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[[plugins]]  package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"  [plugins.inputs.settings]    locale = \"es\" # generates Lighthouse reports in Español\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n...\nSimilar to other build plugins in our ecosystem, the Lighthouse plugin gives you the option to fail your build if your Lighthouse scores fall below defined thresholds. You can set these thresholds within the Netlify .toml file.\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[[plugins]]  package = \"@netlify/plugin-lighthouse\"  [plugins.inputs.thresholds]    performance = 0.9\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n...\nGo install the latest version of the Lighthouse plugin and try out these new features today! This latest release was driven by customer feedback from developers like you. Let us know what you think.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eNow available: manage Build Plugin versions - Updates - Netlify Support Forums\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://answers.netlify.com/t/now-available-manage-build-plugin-versions/42293\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eNow available: manage Build Plugin versions - Updates - Netlify Support Forums\n\n# Now available: manage Build Plugin versions\n\nrstav August 26, 2021, 1:56pm 1\n\nNetlify offers a range of Build Plugins to extend the functionality of the build process, and plugin authors commonly create new versions of plugins to add functionality or address bugs. Today, we’ve released a way to manage the major versions of plugins that your site uses in the Netlify UI.\n\nMajor versions can introduce breaking changes, so we’ve made it easy to both update to a new version as well as roll back to a previous version. Note that Netlify upgrades minor versions in the UI automatically, since minor version updates are backwards-compatible.\n\nTo manage major plugin versions for plugins installed in the Netlify UI, go to the Plugins page for your site (not the team-level plugins directory). You’ll find the Option to Change version for any installed plugins that have version options available. To access a plugin changelog to find out what a new version contains, select Options \u003e Review plugin changelog.\n\nFor plugins installed in using file-based installation, you can manage both minor and major plugin versions in the&#96;devDependencies&#96; in your site’s&#96;package.json&#96; file.\n\nYou can also refer to our Manage plugin version docs.\n\nLet us know what you think or if you have any feedback about this feature!\n\n5 Likes\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",
      "title": "Web search results"
    }
  ],
  "rejected": false
}

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The build failure stems from a plugin version mismatch. Updating the plugin from v4 to v6 via the Netlify UI should resolve it.

File-based installation is the recommended approach. Add the plugin to netlify.toml and install it via npm. This avoids duplication with the UI version. Alternatively, update the version directly in the Netlify UI.

Add `@netlify/plugin-lighthouse` to devDependencies at ^6.0.4 and declare it in `netlify.toml` under `[[plugins]]`. The UI-installed v4 plugin may conflict if left in place, so removing it is likely necessary.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

The Lighthouse plugin is pinned to v4 in Netlify UI, which doesn't support Node 22. I'll switch to file-based v6 in `netlify.toml` and `package.json`.

_**Agent (default)**_

The build failed because **Node 22** (set in `netlify.toml`) conflicts with **`@netlify/plugin-lighthouse@4`** from the Netlify UI, which only supports Node `<20`.

Version **6.x** of the Lighthouse plugin supports Node 20+, so the project is now configured to use file-based installation instead:

1. **`netlify.toml`** — added the plugin block so Netlify uses the repo version
2. **`package.json`** — added `@netlify/plugin-lighthouse@^6.0.4` as a devDependency
3. **`package-lock.json`** — updated via `npm install`

### One more step in Netlify

The old **v4 plugin is still enabled in the Netlify UI** (`origin: ui` in your build log). To avoid running Lighthouse twice or still hitting the old version:

1. Open your site in Netlify → **Integrations** / **Plugins**
2. Find **@netlify/plugin-lighthouse**
3. **Remove** it (or update it to **v6** if that option is available)

After that, redeploy. The build should use Node 22 with Lighthouse 6.0.4 from `package.json`.

If you want, I can commit these changes for you.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Another Netlify build log was shared.

The Lighthouse plugin loaded successfully and the build command completed. The log cuts off before confirming the full deploy succeeded. There may be a hidden failure after the build step.

The fix resolved the Node version conflict. The build command and Hugo build both completed successfully. The log ends without showing the final site deploy status.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

The original issue is resolved. This log shows the fix working:

- **Lighthouse plugin** loads as `@netlify/plugin-lighthouse@6.0.4 from netlify.toml and package.json` (no more Node 22 / `<20` conflict)
- **`npm ci && npm run build`** completes successfully
- **Hugo** builds in ~294ms (2 pages, 4 static files)

Your log stops right after the build command finishes. Netlify typically runs the Lighthouse plugin **after** that as a post-build step, then publishes to `public/`. If the deploy finished, you should see something like “Site is live” and Lighthouse scores in the deploy summary.

If the build failed **after** this point, paste the rest of the log (especially anything after the Lighthouse plugin runs) and I can help with that next step.
